digest.component_or_area: rebar MCP server auth / optional Streamable-HTTP transport
propositions: rebar-mcp is stdio-only with no HTTP transport or authentication, so a shared/hosted rebar store cannot be reached safely by remote MCP clients.

This epic adds an optional Streamable-HTTP transport where rebar acts as an OAuth 2.1 Resource Server validating inbound bearer tokens and advertising RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata, while stdio remains the default and auth is off by default.

A provider-agnostic composite TokenVerifier is the single audience/fail-closed choke point, re-validating every token's resource against a single configured auth_resource_server_url (RFC 8707) since the SDK does not check audience.

Five verifier modes ship (static bearer, JWKS/JWT, RFC 7662 introspection, trusted-proxy passthrough, pluggable custom), each selectable via flat mcp.auth_strategies config and each populating AccessToken.resource.

Unauthenticated requests receive 401 with a WWW-Authenticate header carrying the resource_metadata URL, valid tokens return 200, insufficient scope returns 403, and audience mismatch is rejected at the composite.

Misconfiguration and downgrade are fail-closed: unknown strategy, empty composite with auth on, auth-enabled HTTP with no verifier, or unauthenticated HTTP without explicit ack all refuse to start, and non-loopback bind requires allowlists plus a TLS-at-edge acknowledgment.
